items.prompt: The direction cosines of a directed line L are defined as the cosines of the angles that L makes with which reference?
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.2 Direction cosines

items.prompt: If a line makes angles 90, 60 and 30 degrees with the x, y and z-axes, its direction cosine n = cos 30 degrees has what value (give sqrt(3)/2 as a decimal to 3 places)?
Any three numbers proportional to the direction cosines of a line are called:
items.answer: the direction ratios of the line

expected.correct: the direction ratios of the line
options: the direction ratios of the line
the direction cosines of the line
the direction angles of the line
the position vector of the line

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.2 Direction ratios

items.prompt: A line has direction ratios 2, -1, -2. Its first direction cosine is 2/sqrt(2^2+(-1)^2+(-2)^2). What is the value of that denominator sqrt(4+1+4)?
For the line through P(-2,4,-5) and Q(1,2,3), the distance PQ = sqrt(3^2+(-2)^2+8^2). What is the value under the square root?
To get the direction cosines of the line through two points P and Q from the coordinate differences, you must:
items.answer: divide each difference by the distance PQ

expected.correct: divide each difference by the distance PQ
options: divide each difference by the distance PQ
leave the differences as they are

items.prompt: The vector equation of a line through point with position vector a and parallel to vector b is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.3.1 Vector equation

items.prompt: The Cartesian equation of a line through (x1,y1,z1) with direction ratios a,b,c is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.3 Cartesian equation

items.prompt: The angle theta between two lines is found from their direction vectors using:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.4 Angle between lines

items.prompt: For lines with direction ratios 3,5,4 and 1,1,2, the numerator |a1a2+b1b2+c1c2| = |3*1+5*1+4*2|. What is its value?
Two lines with direction ratios a1,b1,c1 and a2,b2,c2 are perpendicular if:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.4 Perpendicular condition

items.prompt: Lines in space which are neither intersecting nor parallel (and hence non-coplanar) are called:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.5 Skew lines

items.prompt: If two lines in space intersect, the shortest distance between them is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.5 Intersecting lines case

items.prompt: The shortest distance between two lines is defined as the smallest length among segments that:
items.answer: join a point on one line with a point on the other line

expected.correct: join a point on one line with a point on the other line
options: join a point on one line with a point on the other line
lie entirely along the first line
join the origin to each line

items.prompt: For skew lines r = a1 + lambda b1 and r = a2 + mu b2, the shortest distance is given by:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch11 Sec 11.5.1 Distance between skew lines

items.prompt: In Example 9, b1 x b2 = 3i - j - 7k, so |b1 x b2| = sqrt(9+1+49). What is the value under the square root?
